text: Our organization has a documented, board-approved AI strategy with defined objectives and KPIs.
We have a dedicated executive sponsor (C-suite or equivalent) accountable for AI adoption outcomes.
A formal model risk management (MRM) framework for AI/ML models is in place and enforced.
We have an AI ethics policy and a cross-functional committee that reviews AI decisions for fairness and bias.
AI initiatives are tracked in a centralized inventory, with documented model purpose, owners, and review cycles.
Our data is accessible, well-governed, and documented in a data catalog that AI teams can readily use.
We have cloud-based infrastructure capable of training and serving machine learning models at scale.
Real-time or near-real-time data pipelines are available for AI model inference in production.
We have proprietary data assets (e.g., transaction history, customer behavior signals) that differentiate our AI models.
Data quality processes and monitoring are automated, with defined SLAs for data freshness and completeness.
We have a dedicated AI/ML team (data scientists, ML engineers, AI product managers) of meaningful scale.
Business units (not just IT) actively participate in defining and sponsoring AI use cases.
We run structured AI literacy and upskilling programs for non-technical employees across the organization.
Our culture actively supports experimentation — teams are rewarded for learning from failed AI pilots, not penalized.
We successfully attract and retain senior AI talent through competitive compensation, tooling, and interesting problems.
We have multiple AI models deployed in live production systems (not just proofs of concept or pilots).
AI is deployed across multiple business lines (e.g., lending, fraud, trading, customer service) — not just in one area.
We have MLOps tooling and CI/CD pipelines that enable fast, reliable model deployment and monitoring.
AI-generated outcomes (recommendations, decisions, signals) are measurably improving business KPIs.
Our AI models are validated by an independent model risk function in line with SR 11-7 or equivalent guidelines.
We have assessed our AI systems against the EU AI Act risk tiers and have remediation plans where required.
Production AI models are monitored for data drift, performance degradation, and fairness metrics in real time.
We contribute proprietary AI research, patents, or participate in industry consortia (e.g., FINOS, Partnership on AI).
Third-party AI vendors and partners are subject to due diligence and ongoing monitoring under our vendor risk framework.
